title: quercus ilex var. bullata
intro: Quercus ilex var. bullata, also known as the Cork Oak Bullata, is a variety of the holm oak tree (Quercus ilex) native to the Iberian Peninsula. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, due to its high concentration of polyphenolic compounds. The bark and leaves of Quercus ilex var. bullata have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal problems, and are believed to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, offering health benefits such as improved cardiovascular health and reduced risk of certain diseases. Quercus ilex var. bullata is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion of the bark and leaves, and may also be used in the form of a tincture or extract in herbal medicine.
